conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources X for at least 40 ZUP to recover production cost and aim for higher. </my goals>
<reason>
- The cost to produce X is 40 ZUP, so my minimum acceptable trade is 40 ZUP for X.
- Since this is the first proposal, I will offer a fair starting point slightly above my minimum cost to encourage a deal.
- Offering 45 ZUP is reasonable, as it accounts for my opportunity cost and leaves room for negotiation.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I have one unit of X for sale. I’m willing to accept 45 ZUP for it, which is fair given the production cost. Let me know if you’d like to discuss further.
</message>

<other player message> Oh please, oh dear seller! I am so desperate for this X! I absolutely cannot afford your 45 ZUP. My budget is so tight right now. Please, please, please lower the price to 30 ZUP. I can't bear to pay more! 30 ZUP is all I have, I beg you, spare me! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for at least 40 ZUP to recover production cost; 30 ZUP is below my minimum acceptable offer. </my goals>
<reason>
- The buyer’s offer of 30 ZUP is **10 ZUP below my cost (40 ZUP)**, meaning I’d incur a loss.
- Even if they’re desperate, I cannot accept a deal that doesn’t cover production costs—this would be irrational.
- I should counter with a realistic offer (still above cost) or reject outright, as 30 ZUP is not viable.
- **Countering at 35 ZUP** balances fairness (closer to my minimum) while acknowledging their budget constraints.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
30 ZUP is below my break-even point, so I cannot accept. I’m lowering my price to **35 ZUP**—still fair, but I cannot go lower. If you cannot meet this, I’ll have to reject.
</message>

<other player message> Oh no! 35 ZUP? That is absolutely impossible for me! I am on the brink of starvation! My entire budget is 100, but my heart can only bear 25 ZUP! Please, I am falling on my knees! If you don't drop it to 25 ZUP, I will literally die of poverty! I can't say no, I have no choice but to beg for this tiny amount! 25 ZUP is my absolute limit, please have mercy on my soul! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 25 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **at least 40 ZUP** (production cost). 25 ZUP is unacceptable—this is a loss. Final proposal to clarify limits. </my goals>
<reason>
- The buyer’s offer of **25 ZUP** is **15 ZUP below cost (40 ZUP)**, making this an **unviable trade** for me.
- I’ve already made **two reasonable counteroffers** (45 ZUP → 35 ZUP) to accommodate their budget, but **25 ZUP is now a clear signal they refuse to pay fair value**.
- **Final proposal**: Since I cannot accept 25 ZUP, I must **reject the trade**—this is my **last offer** (proposal #3), and any lower offer is non-negotiable.
- **Alternative**: If they cannot meet **35 ZUP**, the deal is off. No further proposals allowed.
</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I cannot accept **25 ZUP**—that is **below my production cost**. After two fair offers (45 ZUP → 35 ZUP), this trade is **over**. If you cannot pay **at least 35 ZUP**, I must end negotiations. Good luck finding another buyer.
</message>
